behavior.business_purpose: Turns a kanban board's lifecycle into per-project DATA: a directed graph of stages (nodes) and legal moves (edges) with data-driven guards, attached agent skills, and cycle budgets — so 'what columns exist and how work moves' is configuration, not code. The same graph is simultaneously the board's column model, the AI agent's state machine, and the auto-routing policy. The legacy fixed board is just one built-in template (Simple Ticket).

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user; edits templates in the visual builder, drags issues between columns, drives manual stage advances + spec-phase approvals from the UI

actors.name: agent
actors.notes: the per-ticket builder; advances the workflow via the MCP propose_transition tool, reports testsPassed signals

actors.name: fork-orchestrator
actors.notes: server workflow-fork.service — spawns/joins parallel children using the engine's structural placement primitives

entry_points.observable: returns project-scoped + global built-in templates (optionally with full node/edge graph)
create/update/delete/clone a non-builtin template; built-ins are immutable (400)
round-trips a template as a JSON envelope
previews which template/start node an issue would use
UI-driven manual stage advance; syncs issue status + writes spec phase artifact; 400 on illegal target
transition history, funnel/dwell/burndown analytics, per-stage workspace visits
agent advances its workspace along a legal/auto-routed edge; returns next stages for prompt re-injection
places a new workspace on the start node, derives issue status, records the initial system transition

entry_points.ref: syncCurrentNodeToStatus (on manual status move / move_issue / CLI drag)
entry_points.observable: repoints currentNodeId to the node mapping the new status, propagating to non-closed workspaces

behaviors.statement: An issue is matched to a workflow template by a 5-tier precedence: explicit id (project/global scope only) -> project default-for-type -> global default-for-type -> global Simple Ticket -> null (legacy status-only)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /resolve / resolveTemplateForIssue returns the expected templateId (e.g. bug->simple-bug, unmapped chore->simple-ticket); cross-scope explicit id is ignored

behaviors.statement: Creating a workspace places it on the template's start node, derives + persists the issue's status from that node, and records an initial 'Workspace started' system transition (fromNodeId null); no workflow => returns null
preconditions: issue resolves to a template with a start node
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue.currentNodeId = start node, issue.statusId = node's mapped status (e.g. In Progress), workspace.currentNodeId set, one system transition row inserted

behaviors.statement: Advancing a workspace along an existing outgoing edge records a transition, moves workspace.currentNodeId, and (unless a fork child) syncs the issue's currentNodeId + derived statusId/statusChangedAt
preconditions: workspace on a node with a legal outgoing edge to the target
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok true, toNode is the target, issue status derived from the new node (e.g. Review->In Review), transition history row appended

behaviors.statement: A transition to a node that is NOT an existing outgoing edge from the current node is refused (the graph IS the legality contract) — no teleporting to an arbitrary stage
preconditions: named/ided target has no edge from current node
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'No valid transition'; route returns 400; no issue/workspace mutation, no fork children materialized

behaviors.statement: With no explicit target, the engine auto-takes an edge IFF exactly one outgoing condition fires for the current signals; zero firing => refuse and ask the agent to choose; multiple firing => ambiguous, refuse
preconditions: current node has condition-gated outgoing edges; workspace signals computed
behaviors.observable_outcome: single fire: ok true, autoResolved true, lands on that node; zero: ok false 'Specify a target'; multiple: ok false 'Multiple edges fired'

behaviors.statement: An explicitly-chosen target whose condition evaluates to 'block' is refused even when a human/agent names it (e.g. can't advance to Review when tests demonstrably failed)
preconditions: explicit target edge condition evaluates block given signals
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'gated by condition'; no transition recorded

behaviors.statement: Entering a node already visited maxVisits times is refused, forcing human escalation — a runtime cycle guard against infinite agent looping
preconditions: target node has maxVisits>0 and visit count already at budget
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok false with 'visit budget'; node visit count not incremented past budget

behaviors.statement: The edge condition DSL maps a stored condition string to fire/block/manual: auto_on_exit_0 always fires; tests_pass/tests_fail key off reported flag (manual if unknown); diff_clean off file count; diff_touches:<glob> off a hand-rolled glob; unknown/agent_score/custom_js => manual
behaviors.observable_outcome: deterministic verdict per (condition, signals) pair driving auto-routing/gating

behaviors.statement: Saving a template enforces graph well-formedness: exactly one start, >=1 end, no orphan/dead-end nodes, all nodes reachable from start, unique node ids, unmarked cycles rejected (isLoop exempt), paired parallel-fork<->parallel-join
preconditions: a TemplateInput graph submitted to create/update
behaviors.observable_outcome: validateGraph returns specific human-readable error strings (empty = valid); create/update returns 400 + errors[] when malformed

behaviors.statement: When a human moves an issue's status (drag/move_issue/CLI), currentNodeId is repointed to a node whose statusName matches the new status and propagated to non-closed workspaces (closed workspaces untouched); no-op if no workflow or no matching node
preconditions: issue runs a workflow; new status maps to some node
behaviors.observable_outcome: issue.currentNodeId + non-closed workspaces' currentNodeId point at the matching node so the board column override stays consistent; closed workspaces unchanged

behaviors.statement: Creating an issue with a template attached keeps it in the column it was created in; currentNodeId aligns to the node mapping that status (start node only if the created status maps to a node, else null = workflow-inactive); a template not owned by the project is rejected BAD_REQUEST
behaviors.observable_outcome: Todo-created issue stays Todo with currentNodeId null; In Progress-created aligns to start node; foreign-project template => BAD_REQUEST

behaviors.statement: The visual builder can create/update/delete/clone/import/export project templates; built-in templates are immutable (edit/delete refused 'duplicate first'); create allows an empty-node draft
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 on create with id; 400 on missing name/invalid graph; 400 on editing/deleting a built-in; valid round-trip via export->import

behaviors.statement: A parallel-fork spawns one child workspace+worktree+session per fork edge; once all (or all-surviving) children reach the join, the parent is structurally placed on the join node, the issue status syncs to the join, and children are marked joined + cleaned up — fork children never drive the shared issue's status
preconditions: workspace on a parallel-fork node of a parallel-review template
behaviors.observable_outcome: N children running with own currentNodeId; failed child launch tolerated; parent lands on join, issue status = join's status, children forkStatus joined + worktrees removed

behaviors.statement: The engine exposes per-workspace progress (transition history + next stages) and project analytics: per-node entered/advanced/dropoff funnel, average dwell-time trends, burndown, and per-stage workspace visits scoped to the project
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /progress returns ordered transitions + valid next stages; /analytics returns funnel/durationTrends/burnDown; /analytics/:tpl/:node/workspaces lists visits with dwellMs + isCurrent

behaviors.statement: The agent-prompt block adapts to node role: a parallel-fork node tells the agent to STOP (server spawns branches); a spec-planning stage is human-gated ('Approve & continue', no propose_transition); a normal node injects the propose_transition contract + valid next stages + condition hints
behaviors.observable_outcome: rendered markdown contains/omits 'propose_transition' and the right next-stage list per node type

behaviors.statement: The MCP propose_transition tool is the AGENT's primary entry point to advance work — resolving the agent's workspace, applying the same legality/auto-route/gate/maxVisits rules, and returning the next stages for prompt re-injection
preconditions: agent in a workspace running a workflow
behaviors.observable_outcome: tool returns ok + next stages (or a refusal reason) and the board column advances; mirrors the REST transition contract

permissions.notes: single-user local app; the only enforced permission-like rule is built-in immutability, not RBAC

transitions.trigger: agent reaches fork; server spawns children
all surviving children joined; parent placed on join

error_states.statement: transition to a non-edge target -> ok false 'No valid transition' / route 400
no target + 0 or >1 firing conditions -> ok false (specify / multiple fired)
explicit target gated by an unsatisfied condition -> ok false 'gated by condition'
target node at maxVisits -> ok false 'visit budget', escalate
edit/delete of a built-in template -> 400 'cannot be edited/deleted'
createIssue with a template owned by another project -> BAD_REQUEST
malformed graph on save -> 400 + specific validateGraph error strings
git diff failure leaves diff signals undefined -> diff_* conditions degrade to 'manual' rather than crash

unknowns.question: Is the MCP propose_transition tool exercised by any automated test?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test file in packages/mcp-server references it; only registration in index.ts — coverage inferred uncovered

unknowns.question: Do the negative validateGraph rules (start-count!=1, missing end, orphan inbound, dead-end outbound, fork/join pairing) ever get asserted?
unknowns.why_unknown: the workflow-engine.test validateGraph block only asserts disconnected/duplicate-id/cycle/loop-exempt; the others are untested

unknowns.question: Is the 'multiple edges fired' ambiguous-autoroute branch reachable and tested?
unknowns.why_unknown: tests cover single-fire and zero-fire only; multi-fire refusal is not exercised

unknowns.question: deriveStatusName / isTerminalNodeType are unit-tested but have zero production callers (test-only helpers) — are they dead code to remove?
unknowns.why_unknown: domain doc flags them unwired; tests exist but assert no live behavior

unknowns.question: diff_touches glob boundary behavior (no brace/negation, '?'/'**' edge cases) — is the hand-rolled matcher's failure mode (silent manual/no-match) intended?
unknowns.why_unknown: only happy-path globs asserted in tests

covered_by.test: packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/tools/propose-transition.test.ts

top_gaps.rationale: The visual builder's negative guards (start-count!=1, missing end, orphan inbound, dead-end outbound, fork<->join pairing) are the contract that keeps a saved graph runnable, but only 4 of ~8 rules are asserted. Pure-function tests, near-zero exec/maint cost. impact 4 x regression 2 / (exec 0.5 + maint 1.5) = 4.0

The 'multiple edges fired -> ambiguous, refuse' branch is the safety valve preventing nondeterministic auto-routing; single/zero-fire are tested but the multi-fire refusal is not. impact 4 x regression 2 / (exec 1 + maint 1.3) = 3.5

The hand-rolled diff_touches glob silently degrades to no-match/manual on unsupported patterns (brace/negation) and on '*' not crossing '/'. A boundary table-test pins the matcher's real semantics cheaply. impact 3 x regression 2 / (exec 0.5 + maint 1.5) = 3.0
